Staff Engineer • Engineering Manager • Backend • Platform

Kaizer Charania

I design backend, platform, and AI-enabled systems that hold up under scale, and I build the team operating systems that keep delivery predictable.

My work sits across distributed systems, event-driven platforms, performance-critical APIs, practical AI retrieval, and engineering leadership. I care about systems that scale technically and teams that scale operationally.

~30s -> <1sScheduler latency~1,200/sec, p99 ~300msNotification platform
See proof View resume

Best fit

The work here is strongest in backend, platform, engineering manager, and technical-lead contexts where system design, team growth, and execution quality all matter.

San Jose, CA 8-engineer team scope Architecture + execution

Proof

Production outcomes that make the technical story easier to trust.

These are the numbers I would expect a hiring team to scan first before deciding whether to go deeper.

Scheduler latency

~30s -> <1s

Notification platform

~1,200/sec, p99 ~300ms

Platform growth

$3M -> $15M MRR

Team scope

8-engineer team

Delivery quality

15% -> 4.97% rework

Current focus

Operating at the intersection of staff-level technical ownership and engineering-manager-level execution: event-driven systems, applied AI retrieval, architecture modernization, mentoring, delivery cadence, and operating standards that scale across a team.

Platform systems Event-driven platform systems

Fanout-heavy backend workflows where throughput, replayability, and delivery isolation matter.

Applied AI AI retrieval and local-first knowledge workflows

Retrieval-backed systems and local-first document intelligence shaped around trust, privacy, and usefulness.

Engineering systems AI-assisted SDLC operating model

Practical guardrails for using AI agents inside software delivery without losing architecture, security, or review control.

Work arc

The scope moved from backend depth into platform ownership and team-scale execution.

1

March 2019 - March 2022

Adara

Software Engineer I -> Software Engineer II -> Lead Engineer

Built backend and data-platform foundations across APIs, event-driven data workflows, identity resolution, and reusable service patterns.

2

March 2022 - Present

Goodyear

Software Engineer Lead

As Software Engineer Lead, improved scheduler latency from roughly 30 seconds to under one second, supported around 1,000 requests per second, helped grow the platform from $3M to $15M MRR, and reduced rework from 15% to 4.97%.

Recent applied AI track

AI Exploration

Extended the platform story into RAG systems, local-first document intelligence, and practical AI-assisted development guardrails.

December 2025 - Present

Case studies

Four proof summaries, one throughline.

Each summary is designed for fast scanning: problem, proof, and the tradeoff behind the work.

Featured · Prior work

Event-driven notification platform

Redesigning synchronous email delivery into a multi-channel MSK pipeline

The original synchronous notifications flow stopped scaling after a major customer onboarding expanded traffic, fanout, and channel requirements. Latency climbed into multi-second territory, some fanout-heavy requests timed out, and users stopped receiving critical updates quickly enough.

~1,200/secp99 ~300msSpikes ~2,500/sec

Tradeoff: Choose MSK over SQS or self-managed Kafka

Read case study

Featured · Recent work

Dynamic scheduling engine

Operational scheduling rebuilt for sub-second decisions

Booking and rescheduling requests were forcing a slow, correctness-sensitive scheduling path that degraded user experience and operational throughput.

~30s -> <1s~1,000 req/sec

Tradeoff: Optimize the whole path, not a single query

Read case study

Featured · Current chapter

AndGo platform scaling and modernization

From legacy delivery constraints to platform-grade execution

Legacy architecture and weak engineering operating systems were slowing delivery, increasing rework, and limiting the platform’s ability to scale with the business.

$3M -> $15M MRR~20% faster delivery15% -> 4.97% rework

Tradeoff: Modernize incrementally instead of pausing for a rewrite

Read case study

Applied AI · Recent work

AI-assisted SDLC operating model

Turning AI coding tools into governed engineering workflows

AI coding tools can accelerate delivery, but without clear repo context, service boundaries, access control, and review discipline, they create hallucinated logic, security risk, and hidden operational cost.

AI SDLCAgent guardrailsRepo context

Tradeoff: Treat AI as an accelerator, not an authority.

On-page proof

Additional signals

Applied AI and platform transition work

Applied AI

Local-first document intelligence

Privacy-first retrieval patterns for personal and operational documents: local ingestion, scoped indexes, source grounding, and reviewable summaries instead of default cloud-first processing.
Local-first AIDocument retrievalPrivacy-first workflow

Applied AI

RAG knowledge retrieval

Retrieval-backed internal knowledge workflows shaped around trust, usefulness, and maintainability rather than chatbot novelty.
RAG systemsAWS BedrockInternal knowledge access

Platform transition

Fleet platform transition

Modernization framed as sequencing work: clarify service boundaries, strengthen review cadence, and keep platform direction tied to delivery while business priorities keep moving.
$3M -> $15M MRR~20% faster delivery15% -> 4.97% rework

Leadership

How I operate when architecture, delivery, and team health are connected.

The pattern in my work is consistent: solve hard backend and platform problems, then improve the engineering systems around them so the team can keep moving with less friction.

Systems over heroics

The goal is durable execution: architecture, process, and team cadence that keep working even when things get messy.

Business-aware engineering

The best projects here are not just technically elegant. They clearly changed reliability, delivery speed, or commercial outcomes.

Practical platform thinking

Enablement matters: templates, GraphQL rollout, onboarding plans, review rigor, and other leverage points that improve how teams build.

Signals

  • I led backend and platform work across an 8-engineer team scope, pairing architecture direction with day-to-day delivery accountability.
  • I introduced stronger design-review rigor, GraphQL and Hasura enablement, onboarding support, and engineering standards that made the team easier to scale.
  • I helped reduce rework from 15% to 4.97% by improving technical quality and execution discipline together instead of treating them as separate problems.
  • My review history consistently points to mentoring, High Potential recognition, and Exceptional Performer ratings.
PythonFastAPI / FlaskREST API designGraphQL / HasuraPostgres / DynamoDB / RedisMicroservicesEvent-driven architectureAWS / GCPDocker / KubernetesRoadmap ownership

AI-assisted SDLC with real guardrails

How to use AI agents for implementation speed while preserving architecture context, review control, security boundaries, and testing discipline.

Event-driven systems under fanout pressure

What changes when synchronous workflows become replayable, channel-isolated platform systems.

Local-first AI retrieval and privacy-first document intelligence

Where document intelligence can stay useful without turning private context into unnecessary cloud dependency.

Platform transition when business priorities change

How to modernize service boundaries and team practices while the business still needs delivery.

Discuss backend, platform, or AI-enabled systems.

I do my best work in environments where architecture, execution, and team effectiveness all need to improve together, not in isolation.
